_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Identify, collect, and seize documentary or physical evidence, to include digital media and logs associated with cyber intrusion incidents, investigations, and operations.
reference_document_element_description: Establish post-deployment AI monitoring standards, including performance threshold definitions, drift detection protocols, incident reporting procedures, and remediation timelines.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI DG domain. DG-T-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-T-010 (RC, cosine: 0.443), AB-T-010 (AB, cosine: 0.437).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.0/10 (raw 0.202). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [associated, collect, cyber, digital, documentary, evidence] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, definitions, deployment, detection, drift, establish] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.858 (P=0.858, R=0.858). Balanced P/R — the 22-word DCWF element and 18-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (100.0%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Identify, collect, and seize documentary or physical evidence, to include digita" and "Establish post-deployment AI monitoring standards, including performance thresho". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.446 > STS 0.202 (gap +0.245). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 2.6%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [associated, collect, cyber, digital, documentary, evidence]. WIDAI terms: [ai, definitions, deployment, detection, drift, establish].
